name: Italian Lasagna (Usda)
recipeIngredient: 18 ounces tomato paste, divided into 3 (6-oz) cans
24 ounces tomato sauce, divided into 3 (8-oz) cans
recipeInstructions:
1. Line four 8x8x2-inch baking pans with heat-resistant freezer wrap. Allow enough extra wrap to fold over the top. Use one pan for each six servings or one-fourth of the recipe. Do not line pans for food to be served without freezing.
2. Crumble beef and cook until lightly browned. Stir in onion. Cook until onion is tender. Drain off fat. Stir in seasonings, tomato paste, tomato sauce, and water. Simmer for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
3. In a separate bowl, blend eggs with ricotta cheese.
4. In each pan, spread a layer of meat mixture (about 3/4 cup). Add layers of noodles (2 1/4), meat mixture (about 3/4 cup), ricotta cheese mixture (about 3/4 cup), mozzarella cheese (1/3 cup), and Parmesan cheese (1 1/2 tablespoons).
5. Repeat layers until all ingredients are used, packing food tightly to avoid air pockets.
6. To serve without freezing: Preheat oven to 400°F. Bake for 30 minutes or until sauce bubbles at edges.
7. To freeze: Cool for 30 minutes at room temperature. Complete wrapping by pulling paper up over top of food. Fold edges together several times so paper lies directly on top of food. Fold ends of freezer wrap over the top and seal with freezer tape. Label with name of food, date of freezing, and last date the food should be used for best eating quality (about 6 months). Freeze for 10-12 hours before removing packages from pans.
8. To heat frozen lasagna: Preheat oven to 400°F. Remove freezer wrap. Place food in a baking pan. Bake for 1 1/4 hours or until sauce bubbles at edges and center is hot.
